title: genproc: deep dive
text: synthetic file-conversion task README — one row per file, convert() per-case function.
code: src_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "genproc-vignette-src") dst_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "genproc-vignette-dst") dir.create(src_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) dir.create(dst_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  write.csv(head(iris),       file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(mtcars),     file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(airquality), file.path(src_dir, "c.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  convert <- function(src_dir, src_file, dst_dir, dst_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(dst_dir, dst_file)) }  mask <- data.frame(   src_dir  = src_dir,   src_file = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   dst_dir  = dst_dir,   dst_file = c("a.rds", "b.rds", "c.rds"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  result <- genproc(convert, mask)

title: genproc: deep dive
text: result S3 list stable, documented set fields: log: one row per case. Columns, order: case_id, mask parameter values (order appear mask), success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs. reproducibility: environment snapshot captured run start (see ). n_success, n_error: summary counts. duration_total_secs: total wall-clock time. status: "done" synchronous run, "running" "error" non-blocking run await(). fields guaranteed stable across minor versions; new fields may added (e.g. worker_id parallel runs), existing ones never removed renamed.
code: class(result) #> [1] "genproc_result" names(result) #> [1] "log"                 "reproducibility"     "n_success"           #> [4] "n_error"             "duration_total_secs" "status"

title: genproc: deep dive
text: Column order designed human scanning run: case_id stable index-based (case_0001, case_0002, …) now. content-based variant roadmap, use cases rows mask can reordered runs.
code: result$log #>     case_id                              src_dir src_file #> 1 case_0001 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src    a.csv #> 2 case_0002 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src    b.csv #> 3 case_0003 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src    c.csv #>                                dst_dir dst_file success error_message traceback #> 1 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst    a.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #> 2 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst    b.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #> 3 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst    c.rds    TRUE          <NA>      <NA> #>   duration_secs #> 1         0.001 #> 2         0.001 #> 3         0.000

title: genproc: deep dive
text: timestamp: POSIXct, start time run. r_version, platform, os, locale, timezone: system info. packages: named character vector package -> version, every package attached loaded via namespace run start. mask_snapshot: exact mask used (copy reference — value). parallel: NULL sequential run, plain list mirroring parallel_spec() used. Dropped class make snapshot portable serialization formats. list also carries effective_strategy: strategy actually applied genproc(), differs strategy user passed workers without explicit strategy genproc() auto-defaulted "multisession". fields recorded snapshot self-explanatory: strategy user asked , effective_strategy applied. nonblocking: pattern, nonblocking_spec(). inputs: fingerprint every file mask refers , NULL input tracking disabled (track_inputs = FALSE). See next section. snapshot lives inside result. can compare two results comparing $reproducibility slots directly.
code: str(result$reproducibility, max.level = 1) #> List of 11 #>  $ timestamp    : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-05-02 18:46:45" #>  $ r_version    : chr "R version 4.6.0 (2026-04-24)" #>  $ platform     : chr "x86_64-pc-linux-gnu" #>  $ os           : chr "Linux 6.17.0-1010-azure" #>  $ locale       : chr "LC_CTYPE=C.UTF-8;LC_NUMERIC=C;LC_TIME=C.UTF-8;LC_COLLATE=C.UTF-8;LC_MONETARY=C.UTF-8;LC_MESSAGES=C;LC_PAPER=C.U"| __truncated__ #>  $ timezone     : chr "UTC" #>  $ packages     : Named chr [1:33] "0.2.0" "0.6.39" "1.4.3" "2.6.1" ... #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:33] "genproc" "digest" "desc" "R6" ... #>  $ mask_snapshot:'data.frame':   3 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ parallel     : NULL #>  $ nonblocking  : NULL #>  $ inputs       :List of 3

title: genproc: deep dive
text: reproducibility$inputs layer protects silent drift upstream files. captured t0 run, alongside rest snapshot. method: currently "stat". Reserved future extensions (e.g. "md5" content hashing). files: deduplicated table every file referenced mask, size bytes last-modified time. One row per unique path: config file shared across 100 cases produces single row. refs: (case_id, column, path) triples saying referenced . Joins back files path.
code: str(result$reproducibility$inputs, max.level = 1) #> List of 3 #>  $ method: chr "stat" #>  $ files :'data.frame':  0 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ refs  :'data.frame':  0 obs. of  3 variables:

title: genproc: deep dive
text: default, every character column mask whose non-NA values existing files (contain path separator) treated input column. mask used vignette paths split across src_dir src_file, heuristic finds nothing useful — src_dir directory (excluded), src_file values bare names (separator). mask holds absolute paths directly:
code: mask_paths <- data.frame(   csv_in = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) do_one <- function(csv_in) nrow(read.csv(csv_in))  run0 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) run0$reproducibility$inputs$files #>                                         path size               mtime #> 1 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv  214 2026-05-02 18:46:45 #> 2 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/b.csv  296 2026-05-02 18:46:45 #> 3 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/c.csv  154 2026-05-02 18:46:45

title: genproc: deep dive
text: Many cases referencing upstream file produce single row files one row per case refs. keeps snapshot economical masks every case shares configuration, schema, lookup table.
code: config_path <- file.path(src_dir, "config.yml") writeLines("threshold: 10", config_path)  mask_with_config <- data.frame(   csv_in = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   config = config_path,                       # same value across rows   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) do_one_cfg <- function(csv_in, config) nrow(read.csv(csv_in))  run_shared <- genproc(do_one_cfg, mask_with_config)  # 4 rows: 3 unique csv_in + 1 config nrow(run_shared$reproducibility$inputs$files) #> [1] 4  # 6 rows: 3 cases x 2 input columns nrow(run_shared$reproducibility$inputs$refs) #> [1] 6

title: genproc: deep dive
text: genproc(..., input_cols = c("col1", "col2")) bypasses heuristic tracks exactly named columns. Paths don’t exist capture time recorded NA size/mtime warning emitted. genproc(..., skip_input_cols = c("col")) keeps heuristic excludes column (useful label column happens match existing file cwd). genproc(..., track_inputs = FALSE) disables tracking entirely. result$reproducibility$inputs NULL. input_cols skip_input_cols mutually exclusive. Mixing raises error — two flags express contradictory intentions call clarify.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: diff_inputs() returns S3 object (genproc_input_diff) print method human reading named list components programmatic access ($changed, $unchanged, $removed, $added). Files matched canonical absolute path; cross-machine comparison need separate matcher scope version. diff_inputs() refuses compare snapshots produced different methods (forward-compatible future hash mode).
code: # Rewrite a.csv with strictly more content (size changes) write.csv(iris, file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  run1 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) diff_inputs(run0, run1) #> genproc input diff (method: stat) #>   Changed:   1 #>   Unchanged: 2 #>   Removed:   0 #>   Added:     0 #>   Cases affected: 1 #>  #> Changed files: #>   /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv #>       size:  214 B -> 3.9 KB #>       mtime: 2026-05-02 18:46:45 -> 2026-05-02 18:46:45 #>  #> Cases affected (use rerun_affected() to re-run): #>   case_0001

title: genproc: deep dive
text: case throws stop run. delete source file two runs: failing row carries error message filtered traceback: traceback captured via withCallingHandlers() moment error thrown — real R call stack, string pulled conditionMessage(). internal tryCatch() withCallingHandlers() frames filtered trace reads like normal R error. Restore file subsequent sections:
code: file.remove(file.path(src_dir, "b.csv")) #> [1] TRUE result_broken <- genproc(convert, mask) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): cannot open file #> '/tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/b.csv': No such file or directory  result_broken$n_success #> [1] 2 result_broken$n_error #> [1] 1 bad <- result_broken$log[!result_broken$log$success, ] bad$error_message #> [1] "cannot open the connection" cat(bad$traceback[1], "\n") #> 1. read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file)) #> 2. read.table(file = file, header = header, sep = sep, quote = quote, dec = dec, fill = fill, comment.char = comment.ch ... #> 3. file(file, "rt") write.csv(head(mtcars), file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

what: Building blocks: extracting f and the mask from a working example
title: genproc: deep dive
text: vignette far assumed f mask already written hand. practice often start working script one specific case want derive parameterized function mask template automatically. Three exported helpers , order.

previous_headings: Building blocks: extracting f and the mask from a working example
title: genproc: deep dive
text: Take example expression works one case. Every external value (string literals, environment symbols functions) becomes parameter resulting function, current value stored default. Locally bound symbols (assignment targets, function formals) protected.
code: # An example that works for ONE specific case input_path  <- file.path(src_dir, "a.csv") output_path <- file.path(dst_dir, "a-from-example.rds")  example <- expression({   df <- read.csv(input_path)   saveRDS(df, output_path) })  fn <- from_example_to_function(example) formals(fn) #> $param_1 #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv" #>  #> $param_2 #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst/a-from-example.rds"

previous_headings: Building blocks: extracting f and the mask from a working example
what: 2. from_function_to_mask() — function signature to mask template
title: genproc: deep dive
text: function, derive one-row template data.frame mirrors signature. can rbind() extra rows build full mask.
code: mask_template <- from_function_to_mask(fn) mask_template #>                                      param_1 #> 1 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv #>                                                   param_2 #> 1 /tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst/a-from-example.rds

previous_headings: Building blocks: extracting f and the mask from a working example
what: 3. rename_function_params() — give the parameters domain names
title: genproc: deep dive
text: auto-generated names (param_1, param_2, …) stable informative. Rename place — formals body updated together, function source edited. Putting together: renamed function plus manually-built mask follows column names. f_mapping argument shown next section inline equivalent step 3 — convenient don’t need renamed function purpose single genproc() call.
code: fn_named <- rename_function_params(   fn, c(param_1 = "input_path", param_2 = "output_path") ) formals(fn_named) #> $input_path #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-src/a.csv" #>  #> $output_path #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpPEA0jg/genproc-vignette-dst/a-from-example.rds" mask_built <- data.frame(   input_path  = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   output_path = file.path(dst_dir, c("a2.rds", "b2.rds", "c2.rds")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) genproc(fn_named, mask_built)$n_success #> [1] 3

title: genproc: deep dive
text: function already uses parameter names don’t match mask’s column names, f_mapping renames place without touching source:
code: # `f` uses generic names; the mask uses domain names. f <- function(input_dir, input_file, output_dir, output_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(input_dir, input_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(output_dir, output_file)) }  genproc(   f = f,   mask = mask,   f_mapping = c(     "input_dir"   = "src_dir",     "input_file"  = "src_file",     "output_dir"  = "dst_dir",     "output_file" = "dst_file"   ) )

title: genproc: deep dive
text: parallel_spec() records intent ; actual workers started lazily future plan resolved.

what: Power-user pattern: manage the plan yourself
title: genproc: deep dive
text: Across many genproc() calls session, installing plan amortizes worker startup:
code: future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 6)  result_1 <- genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec()) result_2 <- genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec()) # reuses the same workers

what: One-off pattern: let genproc install the plan
title: genproc: deep dive
text: workers passed without strategy, genproc() auto-defaults multisession single call restores previous plan exit. avoids silent trap workers = N ignored current plan sequential:
code: genproc(convert, mask, parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4))

title: genproc: deep dive
text: multisession costs roughly 1–3 seconds per worker first use. ~10 seconds real work, parallel run can slower sequential one. Don’t read single short benchmark evidence bug: run workload size.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: parallel_spec(seed = TRUE) (default) draws independent L’Ecuyer-CMRG streams random master seed. Identical master seed — identical per-case RNG state regardless worker count chunking. pin master, pass integer: parallel_spec(seed = 42L).

title: genproc: deep dive
code: job <- genproc(convert, mask, nonblocking = nonblocking_spec()) status(job)         # "running" or "done" job <- await(job)   # blocks until resolution job$log

title: genproc: deep dive
text: await(): log, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs NULL. reproducibility already populated — snapshot captured synchronously, future submitted, reflects t0. status "running". future stored attr(x, "future"). await(): fields populated synchronous run. attr(x, "future") removed. status "done" — "error" wrapper future crashed (rare case: per-case errors caught logging layer don’t propagate wrapper).

title: genproc: deep dive
text: await() idempotent. Calling object already materialized (synchronous begin ) returns unchanged. makes safe pepper user code without tracking whether particular result already collected.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: Unlike parallel_spec(), nonblocking_spec() defaults strategy = "multisession", NULL. Rationale: function named “non-blocking” must silently block current future::plan() sequential (default fresh R session). Power-users manage plan can pass strategy = NULL explicitly defer.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: nonblocking_spec() installs plan, previous plan restored genproc() exit — restored await() future collected. Restoring earlier call future::plan() wrapper future still running, shuts multisession workers surfaces “Future canceled” error collection time. trade-never call await(), installed plan stays active rest session. Power-users pass strategy = NULL manage plan affected.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: two optional layers orthogonal: non-blocking layer launches one outer future. Inside , parallel layer dispatches cases via future.apply. Note: set multisession, future.apply detects already inside future degrades inner layer sequential default, avoid worker explosion. true nested parallelism, install future::plan(list(...)) explicitly pass strategy = NULL specs.
code: job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 6),   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() ) # get control back immediately # ... do other work ... job <- await(job)

what: Default mc.cores in the wrapper subprocess
title: genproc: deep dive
text: Windows RStudio configurations, wrapper subprocess inherits getOption("mc.cores") set 1 (legacy default parallel::mclapply(), -op Windows). Without intervention, parallelly refuse spawn inner workers workers / 1 exceeds localhost hard limit, composed call fail confusing "1 CPU cores available R process (per 'mc.cores')" error. genproc() handles transparently. composed case (parallel != NULL && nonblocking != NULL), makes two adjustments inside wrapper subprocess: Sets R_PARALLELLY_AVAILABLECORES_METHODS = "system" parallelly ignores mc.cores uses true detected core count hard-limit check. Raises options(mc.cores) 1 system core count, parallelly’s soft-limit warning (“1 CPU cores available… 200% load”) fire misleading message hard limit lifted. adjustments apply user set values, inside wrapper subprocess. calling session never modified.

title: genproc: deep dive
text: genproc() integrates progressr framework. Wrap call progressr::with_progress(...) opt : Behind scenes, genproc() emits one progression condition per completed case. progressr lets user pick handler: default text bar console, RStudio gadget, audible beeps, custom log lines, handler user wires via progressr::handlers(). Without with_progress(), integration complete -op — zero overhead, zero visible change. progressr soft dependency declared Suggests; integration skipped package installed. parallel mode, signals worker subprocesses propagated back parent session via future.apply. Live monitoring non-blocking runs yet supported (signals arrive burst await() time rather live run); roadmap.
code: library(progressr)  with_progress(   result <- genproc(my_fn, mask, parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4)) )

title: genproc: deep dive
text: yet package, explicitly planned: Content-hash input fingerprinting: current inputs layer uses stat-based fingerprint (size + mtime), detects every legitimate file modification can fooled adversary preserves . method = "md5" ("xxhash64") opt-reserved API land later. Content-based case_id: today case IDs index-based. content-based variant make replay stable even mask rows reordered. Error replay: replay(result, case_id) rerun one failed case isolation. Live monitoring non-blocking runs: today progressr integration covers sequential parallel paths ; non-blocking path needs different design (collect progress background, query demand) planned. cancel() non-blocking: backend-dependent, deferred. architecture designed adding layers require changes existing user code — new layers composed extra arguments genproc(), extra fields genproc_result accumulate without removing existing ones.

what: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: genproc turns one-iterative R procedures (, lapply(), purrr::pmap(), …) production-grade workflows wrapping orthogonal, composable execution layers. goal make transition “script works one case” “system runs reliably many cases” architectural step, improvised rewrite. Two layers always active disabled: Logged — case produces structured log row real traceback (captured via withCallingHandlers()) per-case timing. Reproducibility — run records R version, loaded package versions, execution environment, exact iteration mask, spec optional layer used, two runs can compared change execution parameters auditable. Two optional layers can composed defaults, caller’s choice: Parallel execution via future ecosystem (future.apply::future_lapply()). Non-blocking execution: genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" run continues background future. Poll status(), block await(). genproc zero Shiny dependency. companion package (genprocShiny) later build UI top functions.

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("danielrak/genproc")

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: toy file-conversion task: read CSVs one directory, save RDS another. One case per file. Every run returns genproc_result — structured list stable shape across runs: log data.frame holds one row per case, case_id, mask parameter values (src_dir, src_file, dst_dir, dst_file ), success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs. display subset columns readability — error_message traceback NA happy path: case fails, run continues — error captured, thrown. point one row mask file exist; b.csv left untouched disk subsequent sections README can still reference : traceback column holds real R call stack failing case (filtered tryCatch/withCallingHandlers machinery), debugging failed case reads like normal R error.
code: library(genproc)  # Synthetic workspace src_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "src") dst_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "dst") dir.create(src_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) dir.create(dst_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  write.csv(head(iris),       file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(mtcars),     file.path(src_dir, "b.csv"), row.names = FALSE) write.csv(head(airquality), file.path(src_dir, "c.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  # Per-case function convert <- function(src_dir, src_file, dst_dir, dst_file) {   df <- read.csv(file.path(src_dir, src_file))   saveRDS(df, file.path(dst_dir, dst_file)) }  # Iteration mask: one row per conversion mask <- data.frame(   src_dir  = src_dir,   src_file = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   dst_dir  = dst_dir,   dst_file = c("a.rds", "b.rds", "c.rds"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  result <- genproc(convert, mask) result #> genproc result #>   Status : done  #>   Cases  : 3 ( 3 ok, 0 error ) #>   Duration: 0.06 secs result$log[, c("case_id", "src_file", "dst_file",                "success", "duration_secs")] #>     case_id src_file dst_file success duration_secs #> 1 case_0001    a.csv    a.rds    TRUE          0.00 #> 2 case_0002    b.csv    b.rds    TRUE          0.02 #> 3 case_0003    c.csv    c.rds    TRUE          0.00 mask_with_missing <- mask mask_with_missing$src_file[2] <- "does_not_exist.csv"  result2 <- genproc(convert, mask_with_missing) #> Warning in file(file, "rt"): cannot open file #> 'C:\Users\rheri\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpS2TaF4/src/does_not_exist.csv': No such #> file or directory result2$log[!result2$log$success,             c("case_id", "src_file", "error_message")] #>     case_id           src_file              error_message #> 2 case_0002 does_not_exist.csv cannot open the connection result2$n_success #> [1] 2 result2$n_error #> [1] 1

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: result$reproducibility plain list recording R version, OS, timezone, loaded package versions, exact mask used, specs optional layer, fingerprint every input file referenced mask. snapshot lives inside result — side file keep sync: taste captured (first package versions):
code: str(result$reproducibility, max.level = 1) #> List of 11 #>  $ timestamp    : POSIXct[1:1], format: "2026-04-29 17:55:37" #>  $ r_version    : chr "R version 4.5.1 (2025-06-13 ucrt)" #>  $ platform     : chr "x86_64-w64-mingw32" #>  $ os           : chr "Windows 10 x64" #>  $ locale       : chr "LC_COLLATE=French_France.utf8;LC_CTYPE=French_France.utf8;LC_MONETARY=French_France.utf8;LC_NUMERIC=C;LC_TIME=F"| __truncated__ #>  $ timezone     : chr "Europe/Paris" #>  $ packages     : Named chr [1:22] "0.1.0" "4.5.1" "1.2.0" "3.6.5" ... #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:22] "genproc" "compiler" "fastmap" "cli" ... #>  $ mask_snapshot:'data.frame':   3 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ parallel     : NULL #>  $ nonblocking  : NULL #>  $ inputs       :List of 3 head(result$reproducibility$packages, 5) #>  genproc compiler  fastmap      cli    tools  #>  "0.1.0"  "4.5.1"  "1.2.0"  "3.6.5"  "4.5.1"

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: result$reproducibility$inputs records size mtime every input file mask refers . intent flag common reproducibility failure: re-running code paths upstream file silently rewritten. genproc detects input columns automatically: character column mask whose values existing files treated . Pass track_inputs = FALSE skip capture, override heuristic input_cols = c(...) (force) skip_input_cols = c(...) (exclude). diff_inputs() compares two runs tells referenced files changed since first one: default method "stat" (size + mtime). detects every legitimate modification near-zero cost; content-hash variant roadmap stronger guarantees adversarial workloads.
code: mask_paths <- data.frame(   csv_in = file.path(src_dir, c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) do_one <- function(csv_in) nrow(read.csv(csv_in))  run0 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) run0$reproducibility$inputs$files #>                                                     path size #> 1 C:/Users/rheri/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpS2TaF4/src/a.csv  221 #> 2 C:/Users/rheri/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpS2TaF4/src/b.csv  303 #> 3 C:/Users/rheri/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpS2TaF4/src/c.csv  161 #>                 mtime #> 1 2026-04-29 17:55:37 #> 2 2026-04-29 17:55:37 #> 3 2026-04-29 17:55:37 # Rewrite a.csv with strictly more content (size changes) write.csv(iris, file.path(src_dir, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  run1 <- genproc(do_one, mask_paths) diff_inputs(run0, run1) #> genproc input diff (method: stat) #>   Changed:   1 #>   Unchanged: 2 #>   Removed:   0 #>   Added:     0 #>  #> Changed files: #>   C:/Users/rheri/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpS2TaF4/src/a.csv #>       size:  221 B -> 4.1 KB #>       mtime: 2026-04-29 17:55:37 -> 2026-04-29 17:55:37

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: Dispatch cases across workers passing parallel_spec(): two mandatory layers remain active parallel mode. Case order preserved log regardless order workers return. hood, parallel_spec() builds configuration object consumed future.apply::future_lapply(). manage future::plan() (recommended across several calls amortize worker startup), pass parallel_spec() without strategy current plan used unchanged.
code: # Four workers, a temporary multisession plan, restored on exit result <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4) )

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: Return immediately, keep console, collect later: nonblocking_spec() can composed parallel_spec() — non-blocking wrapper envelops parallel dispatch:
code: job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() )  status(job)        # "running" or "done" job <- await(job)  # blocks until resolution job$log job <- genproc(   convert, mask,   parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 4),   nonblocking = nonblocking_spec() )

title: Robust, Logged and Reproducible Iteration at Organizational Scale
text: Lifecycle: experimental. four execution layers (logged, reproducibility, parallel, non-blocking) genproc_result contract committed forward compatibility across 0.x series — existing fields guaranteed removed renamed. New fields new optional layers may added. 0.1.0 release first public submission yet CRAN. Install GitHub now (see ).

what: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: Takes function returns modified version : always returns one-row data.frame ("log row") captures errors without stopping, recording error message real traceback (call stack point failure) records wall-clock execution time

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
code: add_trycatch_logrow(f)

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: f function wrap.

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: function formals f, whose return value one-row data.frame columns: original arguments, success (logical), error_message (character NA), traceback (character NA), duration_secs (numeric).

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: tryCatch() unwinds call stack entering error handler. means sys.calls() inside tryCatch error handler returns handler's stack, stack led error. function uses withCallingHandlers() (fires original stack still intact) capture traceback, lets error propagate outer tryCatch() control flow.

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
text: returned data.frame always one row. Columns: One column per formal argument f, value passed success: TRUE body executed without error error_message: error's conditionMessage(), NA traceback: formatted call stack error, NA duration_secs: wall-clock seconds elapsed

title: Wrap a function with error handling and a structured log row — add_trycatch_logrow
code: safe_sqrt <- add_trycatch_logrow(function(x) sqrt(x))  # Happy path: one-row data.frame, success = TRUE. safe_sqrt(4)[, c("x", "success", "error_message", "duration_secs")] #>   x success error_message duration_secs #> 1 4    TRUE          <NA>             0  # Failing call: the run does not stop. The row carries the error # message and a filtered traceback instead of throwing. bad <- safe_sqrt("a") bad[, c("x", "success", "error_message")] #>   x success                                 error_message #> 1 a   FALSE non-numeric argument to mathematical function

what: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: await() blocks background future non-blocking genproc() run resolved, returns genproc_result log, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs, status populated. wrapper future crashed (rare case — user errors inside individual cases caught logging layer wrapper crashes), returned object status = "error" populated error_message.

title: Block until a non-blocking genproc run has resolved — await
text: await() idempotent: calling result already materialized (synchronous begin ) returns unchanged.

what: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: Takes two genproc_result objects produced genproc() (function mask, run two different times) reports referenced input files changed since first run.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
code: diff_inputs(r0, r1)  # S3 method for class 'genproc_input_diff' print(x, ...)

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: r0, r1 Two genproc_result objects. convention, r0 earlier run r1 later one, function symmetric respect changed / unchanged. labels removed (present r0, absent r1) added (opposite) follow asymmetric convention. x genproc_input_diff object. ... Ignored (present S3 method consistency).

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: object class genproc_input_diff (named list) components: method Character, e.g. "stat". changed data.frame columns path, size_before, size_after, mtime_before, mtime_after. One row per file whose size mtime differs. unchanged Character vector paths whose size mtime identical runs. removed Character vector paths present r0's snapshot absent r1's. added Character vector paths present r1's snapshot absent r0's. cases_affected data.frame columns case_id, path, column, change_type (one "changed", "removed", "added"). One row per (case, input column) impacted diff. Pass rerun_affected() re-run impacted cases.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
text: Files matched canonical absolute path. method field must agree two runs.

title: Compare input file fingerprints between two genproc runs — diff_inputs
code: # Two runs of the same procedure, with one input file rewritten # in between. `diff_inputs()` reports the drift. src <- file.path(tempdir(), "diff-inputs-demo") dir.create(src, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) write.csv(head(iris), file.path(src, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  mask <- data.frame(   path = file.path(src, "a.csv"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) read_one <- function(path) nrow(read.csv(path))  r0 <- genproc(read_one, mask)  # Rewrite the file with strictly more rows: size changes. write.csv(iris, file.path(src, "a.csv"), row.names = FALSE)  r1 <- genproc(read_one, mask) diff_inputs(r0, r1) #> genproc input diff (method: stat) #>   Changed:   1 #>   Unchanged: 0 #>   Removed:   0 #>   Added:     0 #>   Cases affected: 1 #>  #> Changed files: #>   /tmp/Rtmp1zJFdc/diff-inputs-demo/a.csv #>       size:  214 B -> 3.9 KB #>       mtime: 2026-05-02 18:46:41 -> 2026-05-02 18:46:41 #>  #> Cases affected (use rerun_affected() to re-run): #>   case_0001

what: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: Takes concrete R expression (e.g. data processing script works one specific case) returns function every external value (strings, variables environment functions) replaced named parameter original value default.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
code: from_example_to_function(expr, env = parent.frame())

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: expr expression length 1, typically created expression() quote() wrapped .expression(). env environment look symbols. Symbols found environment functions turned parameters. Defaults caller's environment.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: function whose body rewritten expression whose formals detected parameters default values. function's environment set env.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: first step genproc workflow: user writes working example, from_example_to_function() extracts reusable, parameterized version .

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: String literals: every string expression becomes parameter (e.g. "output.csv" -> param_1 default "output.csv"). Non-function symbols: symbol exists env value function, becomes parameter.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: Locally bound symbols: variables created assignments inside expression (e.g. result <- ...) never parameterized. Function names: head call (e.g. read.csv read.csv(path)) never parameterized. Functions environment: symbols whose value function assumed part program structure, data. Numeric, logical, NULL, non-character atomic values.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
text: value produces parameter. "output.csv" appears twice, occurrences map param_N.

title: Transform an example expression into a parameterized function — from_example_to_function
code: # --- Basic usage --- # `input_path` exists in this environment; "output.csv" is a # string literal. Both become parameters of the resulting function, # with their original values as defaults. input_path <- "/data/input.csv"  expr <- expression({   df <- read.csv(input_path)   write.csv(df, "output.csv") })  fn <- from_example_to_function(expr) formals(fn) #> $param_1 #> [1] "/data/input.csv" #>  #> $param_2 #> [1] "output.csv" #>   # --- Local bindings are protected --- # `x` is assigned inside the block, so it is NOT parameterized # even though x = 42 exists in the calling environment. x <- 42 expr2 <- expression({   x <- 1   y <- x + 1 }) fn2 <- from_example_to_function(expr2) formals(fn2) #> NULL

what: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: Takes function (typically produced from_example_to_function()) returns one-row data.frame column corresponds parameter, default value cell value. "template mask" starting point user expands multi-row mask defines iteration cases.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
code: from_function_to_mask(f)

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: f function whose formals define mask columns.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: one-row data.frame one column per parameter. Parameters default values get values; parameters without defaults get NA.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: genproc, mask data.frame row iteration case column parameter. function genproc() call user's function per row, passing column values arguments. from_function_to_mask() produces one-row template. user builds full mask adding rows (e.g. via rbind(), dplyr::bind_rows(), constructing multi-row data.frame directly).

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: scalar atomic defaults supported (character, numeric, integer, logical). Non-scalar defaults (vectors, lists, data.frames) supported future version via list-columns. extension preserve backwards compatibility: mask works today continue work unchanged.

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
text: mask returned pure data.frame parameter values. Metadata case_id, input file hashes, seeds managed separately genproc() execution time — stored columns attributes mask. design ensures standard data.frame operations (dplyr::filter(), [, rbind()) never accidentally strip metadata. mask later generalized dedicated class (genproc_mask), existing code passing plain data.frame continue work (backwards compatibility hard constraint).

title: Derive an iteration mask template from a function's signature — from_function_to_mask
code: fn <- function(input_path = "data.csv", n_rows = 100) {   head(read.csv(input_path), n_rows) } mask <- from_function_to_mask(fn) mask #>   input_path n_rows #> 1   data.csv    100 #   input_path n_rows # 1   data.csv    100  # Expand to multiple cases: full_mask <- data.frame(   input_path = c("a.csv", "b.csv", "c.csv"),   n_rows = c(10, 50, 100) )

what: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: genproc package wraps classical iteration constructs (, base::lapply(), purrr::pmap(), ...) orthogonal execution layers one-scripts can turned production-grade procedures without restructuring user's code.

title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: Two layers always active disabled: Logged: case produces structured log row real traceback (captured via withCallingHandlers()) per-case timing. Reproducibility: run records R version, loaded package versions, execution environment, exact mask used, stat-based fingerprint (size + modification time) every input file referenced mask. Silent drift upstream file two runs can detected via diff_inputs(). Two optional, composable layers can enabled per call: Parallel: dispatch cases workers via future ecosystem future.apply::future_lapply(). Configured parallel_spec(). Non-blocking: return genproc_result skeleton immediately run continues background future. Poll status(), block await(). Configured nonblocking_spec(). layers roadmap: monitored progress, error replay, content-hash input fingerprinting, content-based case identifiers. composable default layers opt-. package Shiny dependency designed consumable future companion Shiny package.

title: genproc: robust, logged and reproducible iteration at organizational scale — genproc-package
text: Maintainer: Daniel Rakotomalala rakdanielh@gmail.com

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: central function genproc package. takes function iteration mask (data.frame), calls function per row mask, returns structured result : log data.frame (one row per case, success/error/traceback/timing) reproducibility information (R version, packages, environment, parallel spec) exact mask used stable case IDs linking log rows mask rows

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
code: genproc(   f,   mask,   f_mapping = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   nonblocking = NULL,   track_inputs = TRUE,   input_cols = NULL,   skip_input_cols = NULL )

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: f function apply row mask. formal f correspond column mask (default value). Can produced from_example_to_function() written hand. mask data.frame row iteration case column parameter value. Can produced from_function_to_mask() expanded user. f_mapping Optional named character vector rename f's parameters execution. Passed rename_function_params(). Names current parameter names, values new names matching mask columns. parallel NULL (default, sequential execution) genproc_parallel_spec object produced parallel_spec(). supplied, cases dispatched workers via future.apply::future_lapply(). nonblocking NULL (default, synchronous call) genproc_nonblocking_spec object produced nonblocking_spec(). supplied, genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running", run continues background future. Use status() poll state await() block resolution. Can combined parallel — non-blocking wrapper envelops parallel dispatch. track_inputs Logical. TRUE (default), genproc detects columns mask reference input files records size + mtime result$reproducibility$inputs. Use diff_inputs() compare two runs detect silent input drift. Set FALSE skip input tracking entirely. input_cols NULL (default) character vector mask column names. supplied, heuristic detection bypassed exactly columns tracked. Paths exist capture time recorded NA size/mtime warning emitted. Mutually exclusive skip_input_cols. skip_input_cols NULL (default) character vector mask column names exclude heuristic detection. Useful label column happens match existing file. Mutually exclusive input_cols.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: object class genproc_result (named list) components: log data.frame one row per case. Contains parameter values, plus case_id, success, error_message, traceback, duration_secs. reproducibility list environment information captured run start (R version, packages, OS, locale, timezone, mask snapshot, parallel spec ). See capture_reproducibility(). n_success Integer, number successful cases. n_error Integer, number failed cases. duration_total_secs Numeric, total wall-clock time entire run. status Character. "done" synchronous runs. Future execution layers (non-blocking) may return "running" "error" . genproc_result class designed forward compatibility. Existing fields (log, reproducibility, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs) guaranteed stable. Future versions may add new fields (e.g. worker_id log parallel runs, collect()/poll() methods non-blocking execution) never remove rename existing ones.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: logged reproducibility layers always active disabled. parallel layer optional: pass parallel_spec() parallel enable .

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: Cases executed sequentially row order default. Supply parallel = parallel_spec(...) dispatch parallel via future ecosystem. logging reproducibility layers remain active modes; parallel layer strictly additive. Parallel execution preserves mask row order resulting log data.frame, regardless order workers return. Parallel execution requires genproc installed (loaded via devtools::load_all()) worker, logging layer serializes closures whose environments reference genproc internals. exception parallel_spec(strategy = "sequential"), runs current process needs nothing extra — recommended mode deterministic testing.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: Errors individual cases stop run. case wrapped add_trycatch_logrow(), captures error message real traceback (via withCallingHandlers). run continues next case. holds identically sequential parallel mode.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: genproc() emits one progressr signal per completed case sequential parallel modes. signals -op unless calling code wrapped progressr::with_progress(...), case user sees progress bar (handler chosen via progressr::handlers()):   Without with_progress(), zero overhead zero visible change: integration hook, default behaviour. progressr declared Suggests; integration conditional installation. non-blocking path yet emit signals — live monitoring background runs roadmap.
code: library(progressr) with_progress(   result <- genproc(my_fn, mask, parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4)) )

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: parallel nonblocking supplied, non-blocking wrapper envelops parallel dispatch (one outer future submits run, inner workers process cases). platforms wrapper subprocess R inherits restrictive default getOption("mc.cores") (typically 1 Windows RStudio configurations), parallelly otherwise refuse spawn inner workers. genproc() works around two surgical adjustments inside wrapper subprocess, applied composed case user set values: Set R_PARALLELLY_AVAILABLECORES_METHODS = "system" availableCores() ignores legacy mc.cores option reports true detected core count (lifts hard-limit refusal). Raise options(mc.cores) 1 system core count, parallelly's soft-limit warning longer fires misleading "1 CPU cores available" message. calling session never modified either adjustment.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: row mask receives case_id (currently index-based: case_0001, case_0002, ...). ID appears log can used replay, monitoring, cross-referencing.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
text: mask need contain column every parameter f. Parameters present mask use default values. However, parameters without defaults also missing mask cause error execution starts. Extra columns mask (matching parameter) silently ignored.

title: Run a function over a mask with mandatory logging and reproducibility — genproc
code: # Sequential run (the default). Returns immediately when done. result <- genproc(   f = function(x, y) x + y,   mask = data.frame(x = c(1, 2, 3), y = c(10, 20, 30)) ) result$log #>     case_id x  y success error_message traceback duration_secs #> 1 case_0001 1 10    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0 #> 2 case_0002 2 20    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0 #> 3 case_0003 3 30    TRUE          <NA>      <NA>             0  # One-off parallel call: genproc installs a temporary multisession # plan and restores the previous one on exit. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   result <- genproc(     f = slow_function,     mask = big_mask,     parallel = parallel_spec(workers = 4)   ) } # }  # Non-blocking + parallel composed: launch in the background, # keep the console, collect later with await(). if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   job <- genproc(     f = slow_function,     mask = big_mask,     parallel    = parallel_spec(workers = 6),     nonblocking = nonblocking_spec()   )   status(job)         # "running" until the future resolves   job <- await(job)   # blocks; idempotent on already-resolved jobs   job$log } # }

what: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: Returns configuration object pass nonblocking argument genproc(). supplied, genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" actual work continues background future. Use status() poll state await() block completion.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
code: nonblocking_spec(strategy = "multisession", packages = NULL, globals = TRUE)

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: strategy Character, NULL. One "sequential", "multisession", "multicore", "cluster". Default "multisession". Unlike parallel_spec(), default NULL: function named "non-blocking" must silently block current future::plan() sequential. Pass strategy = NULL explicitly defer caller's plan. "sequential" accepted mainly deterministic testing — exercises code path actually free console. packages Character vector, NULL. Extra packages attach background worker running. genproc attached automatically every strategy "sequential". globals Logical character. Forwarded future::future()'s globals argument. Default TRUE enables automatic detection.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: list class "genproc_nonblocking_spec" validated, normalized fields.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
text: nonblocking_spec() parallel_spec() orthogonal can combined. non-blocking layer launches one outer future; inside , parallel layer dispatches cases via future.apply. strategies set "multisession", future resolves inner layer "sequential" default (see future::plan() nesting rules) unless caller installs explicit nested plan via future::plan(list(...)). Windows RStudio configurations, wrapper subprocess inherits getOption("mc.cores") set 1, lead parallelly refuse inner workers ("1 CPU core available R process"), also emit misleading soft-limit warning even refusal lifted. genproc() works around issues transparently composed case (user set values). See ?genproc details.

title: Specify a non-blocking execution strategy for genproc() — nonblocking_spec
code: # Launch in the background, keep the console if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   spec <- nonblocking_spec()   job <- genproc(f = slow_fn, mask = mask, nonblocking = spec)   status(job)           # "running"   job <- await(job)     # blocks until done   job$log } # }  # Deterministic test: exercise the code path without real async spec <- nonblocking_spec(strategy = "sequential")

what: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: Returns configuration object pass parallel argument genproc(). object describes parallelize; actual execution carried future.apply::future_lapply() top backend selected future::plan().

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
code: parallel_spec(   workers = NULL,   strategy = NULL,   chunk_size = NULL,   seed = TRUE,   packages = NULL,   globals = TRUE )

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: workers Integer >= 1, NULL. Number workers use. Ignored strategy = "sequential". NULL, current future::plan() decides. strategy Character, NULL. One "sequential", "multisession", "multicore", "cluster". NULL (default), current future::plan() used unchanged. specified, genproc temporarily sets corresponding plan run restores previous plan exit. chunk_size Integer >= 1, NULL. Number iteration cases bundled per future. Larger values reduce scheduling overhead cost load-balance granularity. NULL delegates future.apply's default heuristic. seed Controls reproducible random-number generation across workers. Passed future.apply::future_lapply()'s future.seed argument. Default TRUE derives independent L'Ecuyer-CMRG streams random master seed. single integer fixes master seed. FALSE disables reproducible RNG recommended unless user function known RNG-free. packages Character vector, NULL. Extra packages attach worker running user function. genproc attached automatically every strategy "sequential". globals Logical character. Forwarded future.apply::future_lapply()'s future.globals. Default TRUE enables automatic detection, correct almost cases. Set character vector override detection.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: list class "genproc_parallel_spec" validated, normalized fields.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: "sequential": runs current process, workers. Exercises parallel code path without overhead; useful deterministic testing. "multisession": portable (works Windows), launches R subprocesses via parallelly. default recommendation workloads. "multicore": forks current process (Unix/macOS , available Windows reliable inside RStudio). Faster startup multisession loses portability. "cluster": explicit cluster workers, possibly machines. large-scale batch execution. users, leaving strategy = NULL calling future::plan() top session cleanest setup.

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
text: seed = TRUE, case receives independent L'Ecuyer-CMRG stream derived random master seed. master seed -> identical results regardless worker count chunking. pin master seed, pass integer (seed = 42L).

title: Specify a parallel execution strategy for genproc() — parallel_spec
code: # Use whatever plan the caller has set spec <- parallel_spec()  # One-off parallel call with 4 workers, reproducible RNG spec <- parallel_spec(workers = 4, strategy = "multisession",                       seed = 42L)  # Exercise the parallel code path deterministically in a test spec <- parallel_spec(strategy = "sequential")

text: x, invisibly.

title: Print a genproc result — print.genproc_result
text: non-blocking results, status queried live attached future via status() rather read stored field. Repeated print(x) calls therefore reflect actual progress background run. status() distinguishes "done" (future resolved successfully) "error" (wrapper future crashed). Numeric fields stay (pending) await() called materialize result. parallel layer used startup overhead clearly dominated run, print method emits Note hinting issue — pattern often surprises users small workloads. Two metrics depending whether workers known: parallel efficiency ((cumulative / workers) / wall) 50% workers supplied, wall > cumulative * 1.2 power-user mode (workers unknown). require wall > 0.5s avoid noise short runs.

text: body contains nested function definition whose formals shadow parameter renamed, shadowed references inner body still renamed. unlikely practice (parameters from_example_to_function() named param_N) noted completeness.

title: Rename the parameters of a function — rename_function_params
code: fn <- function(param_1 = "in.csv", param_2 = "out.csv") {   df <- read.csv(param_1)   write.csv(df, param_2) }  fn2 <- rename_function_params(fn, c(   param_1 = "input_path",   param_2 = "output_path" ))  # Formals were renamed: formals(fn2) #> $input_path #> [1] "in.csv" #>  #> $output_path #> [1] "out.csv" #>   # And the body too — references to `param_1` and `param_2` are # updated in place, the function source is not edited. body(fn2) #> { #>     df <- read.csv(input_path) #>     write.csv(df, output_path) #> }

what: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
text: Filters original mask r0 cases referenced inputs reported changed, removed, added diff_inputs(), re-runs genproc() subset.

title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
code: rerun_affected(   r0,   diff,   f,   parallel = NULL,   nonblocking = NULL,   track_inputs = TRUE,   input_cols = NULL,   skip_input_cols = NULL )

title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
text: r0 genproc_result produced genproc(). $reproducibility$mask_snapshot provides original mask; must contain track_inputs = TRUE (default). diff genproc_input_diff produced diff_inputs(). f function. Typically function passed original genproc() call. result object store f, must supplied . parallel, nonblocking, track_inputs, input_cols, skip_input_cols Forwarded genproc() re-run. default, inherit sensible behaviour: track_inputs = TRUE (re-run comparable), arguments default NULL (sequential, blocking, automatic input tracking).

title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
text: new genproc_result covering affected cases. case_ids local subset (re-numbered starting case_0001); link back original r0 via matching rows r0$reproducibility$mask_snapshot. diff reports affected cases, function returns NULL message — nothing re-run.

title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
text: actionable end reproducibility layer: upstream file silently drifts, need re-run whole mask. rerun_affected() produces smaller run refreshes impacted outputs.

title: Re-run only the cases impacted by an input diff — rerun_affected
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   r0 <- genproc(my_fn, my_mask)   # ... time passes, some upstream files change ...   r1 <- genproc(my_fn, my_mask)    d <- diff_inputs(r0, r1)   # d$cases_affected lists the case_ids whose inputs drifted.    refreshed <- rerun_affected(r0, d, f = my_fn)   refreshed$log } # }

what: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
title: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
text: Filters original mask r0 cases success == FALSE re-runs genproc() subset. Useful transient external problem caused cases fail user fixed cause: rather re-running whole mask, failed cases refreshed.

title: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
code: rerun_failed(   r0,   f,   parallel = NULL,   nonblocking = NULL,   track_inputs = TRUE,   input_cols = NULL,   skip_input_cols = NULL )

title: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
text: r0 genproc_result produced genproc(). $reproducibility$mask_snapshot provides original mask. f function. Typically function passed original genproc() call. result object store f, must supplied . previous failures caused bug f, pass corrected version. parallel, nonblocking, track_inputs, input_cols, skip_input_cols Forwarded genproc() re-run.

title: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
text: new genproc_result covering failed cases. case_ids local subset (re-numbered starting case_0001); link back original r0 via matching rows r0$reproducibility$mask_snapshot. r0 zero failed cases, function returns NULL message — nothing re-run.

title: Re-run only the cases that failed — rerun_failed
code: r0 <- genproc(   f = function(x) if (x %% 2 == 0) stop("even") else x,   mask = data.frame(x = 1:6) ) # 3 cases failed (the even ones). After fixing f, retry only those: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   r1 <- rerun_failed(r0, f = function(x) abs(x))   r1$log } # }

what: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
title: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
text: status() non-blocking S3 generic. genproc_result, returns one : "running" — underlying future yet resolved. "done" — future resolved successfully (result ready collected via await()). "error" — wrapper future crashed. Call await() retrieve error message.

title: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
code: status(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'genproc_result' status(x, ...)

title: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
text: x object. Methods exist genproc_result. ... Unused, future extensions.

title: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
text: single character string: "running", "done", "error".

title: Query the status of a genproc run without blocking — status
text: synchronous (non-nonblocking) result, status() simply returns result$status ("done" "error"). status() peeks resolved future via future::value() inside tryCatch. value() consumes future, peek result cached shared environment subsequent await() re-materialize .

title: Summarise a genproc result — summary.genproc_result
text: Produces compact digest run: status, success rate, duration stats, top recurring error messages. Useful runs lot cases raw log noisy eyeball.

title: Summarise a genproc result — summary.genproc_result
code: # S3 method for class 'genproc_result' summary(object, top_errors = 10L, ...)

title: Summarise a genproc result — summary.genproc_result
text: object genproc_result produced genproc(). top_errors Integer. Maximum number distinct error messages include summary, ranked occurrence. Default 10. ... Unused, future extensions.

title: Summarise a genproc result — summary.genproc_result
text: object class genproc_result_summary (list) components: materialized Logical. FALSE run non-blocking collected via await(). case fields NA. status Character, mirrors result$status. n_cases Integer. n_success, n_error Integers. success_rate Numeric 0..1. duration_total_secs Numeric, wall-clock total. duration_stats List total, mean, max, slowest_case_id. NULL per-case durations. top_errors data.frame columns error_message count, sorted count descending. Trimmed top_errors rows.

title: Summarise a genproc result — summary.genproc_result
code: result <- genproc(   f = function(x) {     if (x %% 2 == 0) stop("even")     if (x %% 3 == 0) stop("multiple of three")     x   },   mask = data.frame(x = 1:12) ) summary(result) #> genproc result summary #>   Status     : done #>   Cases      : 12 (4 ok, 8 error) #>   Success    : 33% #>   Total time : 0.02s #>   Per case   : mean 0.000s, max 0.001s (slowest: case_0002) #>  #> Top errors: #>     6x  even #>     2x  multiple of three

title: genproc 0.2.0
text: genproc() now integrates progressr framework. calling code wrapped progressr::with_progress(...), one progression signal emitted per completed case (sequential parallel modes; signals worker subprocesses propagated future.apply). user picks handler (text bar, RStudio gadget, beeps, custom) via progressr::handlers(). Without with_progress(), integration complete -op. progressr Suggests; integration skipped installed. Live monitoring non-blocking runs roadmap. New errors(result) returns failed-case rows log original columns (case_id, mask params, error_message, traceback, duration_secs). Replaces boilerplate result$log[!result$log$success, ] pattern. New summary(result) (S3 method genproc_result) produces compact human-readable digest: status, success rate, per-case duration stats (mean, max, slowest case_id), top recurring error messages occurrence (configurable via top_errors). Useful runs many cases raw log noisy eyeball. New rerun_failed(r0, f) helper. Sibling rerun_affected(): filters original mask cases failed re-runs genproc() subset . Useful fixing cause transient failure. New rerun_affected(r0, diff, f) helper. Closes reproducibility loop: [diff_inputs()] reports drift two runs, rerun_affected() filters original mask cases referenced impacted files re-runs genproc() subset . resulting genproc_result small refresh, full re-run. diff_inputs() now returns new $cases_affected field: data.frame columns case_id, path, column, change_type listing every (case, input column) pair impacted diff. Available programmatically input rerun_affected(). print method also shows concise summary (“Cases affected: N”) hint towards rerun_affected(). print.genproc_input_diff now distinguishes small size variations whose human-readable rounding identical: formatted size sides, byte delta shown explicitly (size: 1.1 KB -> 1.1 KB (+6 B)).

title: genproc 0.2.0
text: result$reproducibility$parallel now carries effective_strategy field alongside user-requested strategy. two differ user passed workers without explicit strategy, case genproc() auto-defaults "multisession"; snapshot now records , preserving audit trail requested vs applied. Mode line print(result) now shows effective strategy default, sequential vs parallel multisession run longer ambiguous printed summary. status() now distinguishes "done" (wrapper future resolved successfully) "error" (wrapper crashed), even [await()] called. Previously status() returned "done" soon future resolved, regardless outcome — leading misleading Status: done (collected) print job actually failed. peek result cached shared environment subsequent await() re-materialize future. print(result) informative: Started line shows run’s timestamp, Mode line summarises execution configuration (sequential, multisession parallel (4 workers), non-blocking + multisession parallel (6 workers), etc.), method emits errors(x) / summary(x) hints failures occurred. non-blocking print also distinguishes done (collected) error (collected). parallel used startup overhead clearly dominated run, print(result) now emits Note warning. Two metrics: parallel efficiency 50% workers supplied (catches cases like parallel_spec(workers = 4) yield real speedup), wall-clock cumulative * 1.2 power-user mode (workers unknown). require wall > 0.5s avoid noise. Addresses common surprise activating parallel small workload observing slowdown. Tracebacks captured logged layer now substantially shorter easier read. Internal dispatcher frames (execute_cases, .call, FUN), invocation context frames (source, eval, withVisible), PSOCK worker frames (workRSOCK, workLoop, workCommand, makeSOCKmaster) now dropped head stack, first surviving frame always user code. User calls lapply() .call() within function preserved (head-position filter consumes leading frames). Composing parallel = parallel_spec(...) nonblocking = nonblocking_spec(...) now works box Windows RStudio configurations wrapper subprocess inherits getOption("mc.cores") set 1. Previously, composed call failed parallelly “1 CPU cores available” error, (less visibly) emitted misleading soft-limit warning. genproc() now applies two surgical adjustments inside wrapper subprocess composed case (user set values): sets R_PARALLELLY_AVAILABLECORES_METHODS = "system" lift hard limit, raises options(mc.cores) silence soft-limit warning. calling session never modified.

title: genproc 0.1.0
text: First public release. package consolidates four execution layers (logged, reproducibility, parallel, non-blocking) building blocks (from_example_to_function(), from_function_to_mask(), rename_function_params(), add_trycatch_logrow()) stable API contract. genproc_result S3 class fields guaranteed forward-compatible across 0.x series.

title: genproc 0.1.0
text: Logged — structured log real traceback (captured via withCallingHandlers()) per-case timing. Reproducibility — environment snapshot run start (R version, platform, loaded package versions, mask, specs optional layer used). New parallel_spec() parallel argument genproc(): optional parallel dispatch future.apply::future_lapply(). Auto-defaults "multisession" workers passed without explicit strategy, restoring previous plan exit. New nonblocking_spec() nonblocking argument genproc(): genproc() returns immediately genproc_result status "running" run continues background future. Use status() poll, await() block resolution. Composable parallel. reproducibility layer now records stat-based fingerprint (size + mtime) every input file referenced mask. Stored result$reproducibility$inputs (method, files, refs). Heuristic detection default; explicit override via genproc(..., input_cols = ...) skip_input_cols = .... Disable track_inputs = FALSE. New diff_inputs(r0, r1) compares input fingerprints two runs reports changed / unchanged / added / removed files, human-readable print method.

title: genproc 0.1.0
text: New S3 class genproc_result stable fields: log, reproducibility, n_success, n_error, duration_total_secs, status. Per-case errors stop run; captured log surfaced n_error. case_ids index-based (case_0001, …) now; content-based variant planned.

title: genproc 0.1.0
text: from_example_to_function(): turn example expression works one case parameterized function. String literals free symbols become parameters original value default. Built dependency-free AST rewriter. from_function_to_mask(): derive one-row template data.frame function’s signature, ready expanded full iteration mask. rename_function_params(): rename parameters formals body one pass, without editing function source. add_trycatch_logrow(): standalone logging wrapper used genproc(), exposed users want logged layer outside full pipeline.
